title: Draft
description: Validates a draft artifact — the written reported stories of an issue, each claim attributed and its confidence declared. Produced by write; consumed by factcheck and publish.

issue_id.description: The issue this draft belongs to.

stories.description: The written reported stories — one per selected candidate.

candidate_id.description: The dispatch candidate this story reports — the traceability link to lineup and dispatch.

kind.description: Echoes the candidate's kind. An archive_gap story reports a materially relevant silence in the record.

claims.description: The factual claims the story asserts, each attributed to a source. For an archive_gap story, the claims assert the existence and extent of the gap, grounded in the evidence of absence.

claim_id.description: Stable identifier, referenced by grounding verdicts.

statement.description: The factual assertion the story makes.

source_locator.description: The corpus source this claim rests on. The grounding floor: every claim — firm or qualified — carries a source. Confidence never substitutes for a source.

confidence.description: firm = the source fully supports the statement as asserted. qualified = the source supports the statement only partially or indirectly; the statement is hedged and its limits are stated. Confidence declares the strength of the grounding; it never excuses its absence.

limits.description: Required in spirit whenever confidence is 'qualified': what the source does not establish, so the uncertainty is visible to the reader rather than hidden. Omitted for a firm claim.
